data.title: Inuvo to discuss Q2 2025 results in conference call on August 7 at 4:15 PM ET (NYSE: INUV). Update on AI AdTech solutions.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, Inuvo, Inc. (NYSE American: INUV), a prominent artificial intelligence AdTech solutions provider based in Little Rock, Ark., announced that it will conduct a conference call on Thursday, August 7, 2025, at 4:15 PM Eastern Time. The purpose of the call is to review the financial outcomes and share business insights for the second quarter concluding on June 30, 2025.

data.title: Teramount secures $50M in Series A led by KDT to boost AI infrastructure optical connectivity prod'n.
data.description: Koch Disruptive Technologies (KDT) is spearheading the Series A funding round to expedite Teramount's shift towards high-volume production. Other prominent investors involved in this funding round include AMD Ventures, Hitachi Ventures, Samsung Catalyst Fund, Wistron, Grove Ventures, and various other contributors. This funding initiative is crucial for Teramount as it will aid in maximizing production levels, marking a significant advancement for the company.

data.title: Moleaer introduces breakthrough nanobubble process improving battery range, hydrogen production, water filtration efficiency.
data.description: Recently released patent discloses an eco-friendly method to enhance the porosity of thin films in applications related to energy, water, and clean technology. Moleaer, based in HAWTHORNE, Calif., revealed this chemical-free technique. The announcement was made on July 31, 2025, by the global nanobubble technology leader.

data.title: NetraMark to attend Canaccord Genuity Growth Conference; CEO to attend on August 12–14, 2025 in Boston.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, NetraMark Holdings Inc. (CSE: AIAI) (OTCQB: AINMF) (Frankfurt: PF0), a leading artificial intelligence company revolutionizing clinical trials, revealed that its CEO, George Achilleos, will be participating in the 45th Annual Growth Conference organized by Canaccord Genuity. The event will be held from August 12 to 14, 2025, in Boston, Massachusetts.

data.title: Dan Watson promoted to Senior VP, Managing Director of Vortex International, a trenchless solutions provider.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, in Houston, a leading provider of trenchless infrastructure solutions, the Vortex Companies, promoted Dan Watson from Managing Director of McAllister (a subsidiary) to Senior Vice President. This move highlights the company's growth and commitment to advancing talent within its organization. This decision reflects Vortex's dedication to recognizing and nurturing its top-performing employees. Watson's new role will involve overseeing key operations and strategic management, contributing to the company's continued success.

data.title: STRATA Skin Sciences will report Q2 2025 financial results on August 13, 2025.
data.description: Horsham, Pennsylvania – On July 31, 2025, STRATA Skin Sciences, Inc. (“STRATA” or the “Company”) (NASDAQ: SSKN), based in Horsham, PA, a medical technology company focused on developing, commercializing, and marketing innovative products for dermatologic conditions, declared that it is set to disclose its second quarter 2025 financial outcomes on Wednesday, August 13, 2025, post the market closure. This report will provide insight into the financial performance of STRATA Skin Sciences, Inc., allowing stakeholders and investors to assess the company's progress and economic standing. This news reflects the company's commitment to transparency and keeping shareholders informed.

data.title: NREF reports $12.3M net income for Q2 2025, provides guidance for Q3 2025.
data.description: In the latest financial report until June 30, 2025, NexPoint Real Estate Finance, Inc. (NREF) disclosed a net income of $12.3 million designated for common stockholders, equivalent to 0.54 per diluted share. The company trades on the NYSE under the ticker symbol NREF.

data.title: Profusa signs Letter of Intent with Dismeval, S.L. to distribute Lumee Oxygen platform in Spain, focusing on medical equipment.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, Profusa, Inc. revealed that it has signed a Letter of Intent (LOI) to form a distribution partnership in Spain for its Lumee Oxygen platform. The agreement is with Dismeval, S.L., a Spanish distributor known for handling medical and surgical equipment across Spain, including the Canary and Balearic Islands. Dismeval, S.L. boasts a proficient team with extensive experience in the medical device industry and is well-equipped to provide support for hospital-based clinical and technical operations. Profusa, a digital health company traded on NASDAQ under PFSA, is renowned for its innovative technology that allows for constant monitoring of an individual’s biochemistry.

data.title: Navigator Gas to release Q2 results on August 12, 2025, for the three months ending June 30, 2025 - the company owns world's largest fleet of handysize liquefied gas carriers.
data.description: In London on July 31, 2025, Navigator Holdings Ltd. (NYSE: NVGS), known as Navigator Gas, revealed plans to unveil its financial outcomes for the quarter concluding on June 30, 2025. The company, in possession of the most extensive collection of small-sized liquefied gas carriers globally, is scheduled to disclose these results post the closing of markets in New York on Tuesday, August 12, 2025.

data.title: Peoples Financial Services Corp. reported a Q2 2025 net income of $17.0 million (EPS $1.68), up from $15.0 million in Q1. Overall, net income for H1 2025 was $32.0 million, compared to $6.7 million in H1 2024. Key metrics like ROAA and ROAE improved, attributed to higher net interest income and lower credit losses provisions. The company completed a merger, leading to increased assets, income, and efficiencies. Dividend increased by 50.6% in Q2 2025, significant balance sheet growth noted. The company maintained solid asset quality. comprehensive financial details available on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Peoples Financial Services Corp. reported net income of $17.0 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, with a net income of $32.0 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025. This increase was attributed to higher net interest income and noninterest income, as well as a lower provision for credit losses. The company completed a merger in July 2024, leading to increased interest-earning assets and transaction volumes. Key metrics include a return on average assets of 1.36%, return on average equity of 13.87%, and an efficiency ratio of 53.92% for the three months ended June 30, 2025. Notable figures include total assets, loans, and deposits at $5.1 billion, $4.0 billion, and $4.3 billion respectively at June 30, 2025. The company's stockholders' equity equaled $494.1 million or $49.44 per share at June 30, 2025. Nonperforming assets were $17.4 million or 0.44% of loans, net, and foreclosed assets at June 30, 2025. Additionally, the Company redeemed $33.0 million of its subordinated notes and issued $85.0 million in new notes during June 2025.

data.title: Q2 2025: Patrick Industries' net sales increased 3% to $1.05 billion, with net income at $32 million and adjusted diluted EPS at $1.50. Adjusted EBITDA increased 4% to $135 million. Cash flow from operating activities grew to $189 million, and free cash flow was $262 million. Share repurchases totaled over $23 million, and regular dividends amounted to $13 million. Total net liquidity was $835 million, and a total net leverage ratio of 2.6x was reported. Operating income increased 2% to $87 million. There was a legal settlement related to a motor vehicle accident, resulting in a double-fatality. A webcast on their earnings conference call scheduled on July 31, 2025. Total debt stood at approximately $1.3 billion, with available liquidity at $835 million. The company remained disciplined in capital allocation. RV revenue increased 7%, Marine revenue decreased 1%, Powersports revenue decreased 7%, and Housing revenue increased 3%. Operating income was $87 million, with a margin of 8.3%. Comparable financial data from Q2 2024 provided for better understanding. Full financial data and forward-looking statements available in the release. Full story available on Benzinga.com.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, Patrick Industries, a component solutions provider for the Outdoor Enthusiast and Housing markets, reported a 3% increase in net sales to $1.05 billion. This growth was driven by a 7% increase in the RV business and a 3% increase in Housing business, offsetting reductions of 1% and 7% in Marine and Powersports businesses, respectively. Operating income increased 2% to $87 million, with operating margin at 8.3%. Net income was $32 million, with adjusted net income at $51 million and adjusted diluted EPS at $1.50. Adjusted EBITDA increased 4% to $135 million, and cash flow provided by operating activities grew to $189 million. The company returned $36.5 million to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends. Total net liquidity was $835 million, while total debt was approximately $1.3 billion, resulting in a total net leverage ratio of 2.6x. The company remains optimistic about market resilience and strategic growth opportunities. Jeff Rodino, President - RV, emphasized the company's focus on operational adjustments, new product development, and acquisitions to capitalize on market conditions. The company's revenue by market sector in the second quarter of 2025 showed growth in the RV and Housing sectors, while Marine and Powersports sectors experienced declines. Patrick Industries remains committed to driving profitable growth and value for stakeholders while adapting to changing market dynamics.

data.title: Baylin Technologies to discuss financial results for three and six months ended June 2025 on August 7, 2025 at 10 a.m.

data.title: Second patient with acute myeloid leukemia dosed with CER-1236, promising update on first patient, protocol evaluations initiated.
data.description: The second patient in the initial group is currently undergoing scheduled assessments as the Company delivers positive news on the pharmacokinetic findings for the first patient.

data.title: ARRIS appoints Dolby CEO Kevin Yeaman to its board of directors, bringing expertise in licensing and technology scaling.
data.description: Arris, a top player in advanced manufacturing for high-performance composites, has recently named Kevin as their new CEO. With a background in licensing, brand-building, and technology scaling from his time as CEO of Dolby, Kevin is expected to bring valuable expertise to support Arris' growth. The appointment was made public in Berkeley, California, on July 31, 2025. This move signifies Arris' focus on further expanding their market presence and enhancing their technological capabilities under Kevin's leadership.

data.title: Ashley Swain to offer advanced PMU training for experienced pros in Palm Beach Gardens, FL in fall 2025. 30-year expert.
data.description: Renowned PMU specialist Ashley Swain has announced the launch of an innovative training program tailored for skilled professionals in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida. This initiative was unveiled on July 31, 2025, at Ashley Swain Permanent Makeup & Medspa, an esteemed establishment catering to permanent makeup enthusiasts in Palm Beach County.

data.title: Home prices drop in 14 major U.S. metros, including Oakland (-6.8%), West Palm Beach (-4.9%) and Houston (-2.8%).
data.description: In Seattle, the median home-sale price dropped in 14 of the 50 most populous U.S. metro areas this week, as per a recent report from Redfin. The largest decline was observed in Oakland, CA, with a significant 6.8% year-over-year decrease. Following closely were West Palm Beach (-4.9%), Jacksonville (-3.1%), Austin (-2.9%), and Houston (-2.8%), all experiencing notable reductions in prices. The sluggish housing market is attributed to the high house inventory in these areas.
